highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.139: Agent View & /goal Command
highlights.summary: Claude Code v2.1.139 introduces two major features: the Agent View (Research Preview), accessible via `claude agents`, which shows all your sessions in one unified list whether they're running, blocked, or done. The new `/goal` command lets you set a completion condition so Claude keeps working autonomously across turns until the goal is met — great for longer, hands-off tasks.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.140: Case-Insensitive Agent Matching & /goal Bug Fix
highlights.summary: The latest release (v2.1.140) makes the Agent tool more forgiving by accepting case- and separator-insensitive `subagent_type` values, so `'Code Reviewer'` now correctly resolves to `code-reviewer` without errors. It also fixes a silent hang in the `/goal` command that occurred when `disableAllHooks` or `allowManagedHooksOnly` was set.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest addition to the Opus model family. Developers using Claude Code can target this model for the most capable tasks, making it worth exploring for complex coding projects and multi-step agent workflows.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial promises a fast, focused overview of Claude Code designed to get developers productive quickly. If you're already past the basics and want a concise deep-dive into core workflows, this 30-minute format is an efficient way to level up without watching hours of content.

highlights.title: Full Claude Code Tutorial for Beginners (2026)
highlights.summary: A step-by-step beginner tutorial for Claude Code in 2026, walking non-technical users through everything from setup to practical use. If you're just getting started with Claude Code in VS Code, this video is a good first stop to understand the fundamentals before exploring advanced features.

highlights.title: Show HN: adamsreview – Multi-Agent PR Reviews for Claude Code
highlights.summary: adamsreview is an open-source Claude Code plugin that runs deeper pull request reviews using parallel sub-agents, validation passes, and persistent JSON state — and can optionally combine results from Codex CLI for ensemble reviews. The author reports it catches significantly more real bugs than Claude's built-in review, making it a compelling addition to any CI workflow.

highlights.title: Show HN: Solo Developer Builds 500K LOC Production App in 7 Months with AI
highlights.summary: A developer shares how they built a 500,000-line production application entirely alone in 7 months, with a 5-hour uncut live coding session as proof. The post sparked active Hacker News discussion about the real-world productivity ceiling of AI-assisted coding tools like Claude Code, and what workflows make such output possible.

tips_of_the_day: Use the new `/goal` command to describe a clear, measurable outcome (e.g. '/goal: all tests pass and no TypeScript errors') — Claude will loop autonomously until the condition is met, freeing you from babysitting long tasks.

When configuring sub-agents in your CLAUDE.md or tool calls, don't worry about exact casing — as of v2.1.140, 'Code Reviewer', 'code_reviewer', and 'code-reviewer' all resolve to the same agent, so your configs are more portable.

Before diving into a 2-hour tutorial, try the 30-minute 'Mastering Claude Code' format first — identifying one specific gap in your knowledge and searching for a focused resource will accelerate your learning faster than watching tutorials end-to-end.
